tactical_shape: Controlled spell with minimal second-half threat; relied on Messi’s moments rather than sustained pressure
standout_players: Lionel Messi — scored twice, broke the World Cup scoring record, and was the decisive figure
Emiliano Martínez — repelled a Marcel Sabitzer free-kick
Thiago Almada — intelligently let the ball run for Messi’s record-breaking goal
Leandro Paredes — found Messi for the second goal
Julián Álvarez — had an effort saved in the buildup to Messi’s opener
Lautaro Martínez — won the penalty via the VAR-reviewed foul
home_read.momentum: Argentina keep riding Messi’s historic brilliance and carry the aura of a side that can still turn an otherwise blunt match into a memorable result.

tactical_shape: Crisp passing and a ferocious, scrappy press
Pushed for an equaliser in the second half
standout_players: Ralf Rangnick’s side were described as competent but blunt
David Alaba — nearly turned a Messi dribble into an own goal
Xaver Schlager — caught in the challenge that preceded Messi’s opener
Marcel Sabitzer — had a free-kick saved by Emiliano Martínez
Patrick Wimmer — glanced a late chance wide
away_read.momentum: Austria showed enough pressing and passage of play to trouble Argentina, but the defeat leaves them powerless in the face of Messi-inspired destiny.
